प琇रभाषाएं, यांि灴क वाहन犘 क琉 炈ेणी, इनके अनुम瀄य भार, छूट 灹ा瀉त वाहन犘 क琉 炈ेणी, स琔वस रोड, वैकि瀖पक सड़क इ瀄या琈द किथत िनयम犘 म犆 िविन琖द瀠ट प琇रभाषा猒 के अनु牲प ह犘गी । 8. फ琉स क琉 दर犆 किथत िनयम के िनयम 12 के अनुसरण म犆 灹यो榉ता猒 के सूचनाथ榁 灹द琔शत करनी ह犘गी । 9. यांि灴क वाहन犘 से फ琉स क琉 वा瀡तिवक रािश तािलका-1 म犆 यथा-उि瀖लिखत आधार वष榁 2007-08 के िलए 灹ित 琈कमी फ琉स क琉 आधार दर के आधार पर 灹भा琇रत होगी और छूट犘 क琉 गणना प琇रयोजना राजमाग榁 के पूण榁 हो चुके भाग क琉 लंबाई के आधार पर 灹ािधकरण 獧ारा क琉 जाएगी । 灹ािधकरण 獧ारा इसे 琇रयायत灡ाही के मा瀇यम से 灹瀄येक अं灡ेजी और देशी भाषा म犆 कम से कम एक समाचार प灴 म犆 灹कािशत कराया जाएगा और त瀄प瀙चात् किथत िनयम犘 के िनयम 5 और 琇रयायत करार के उपबंध के अनुसरण म犆 वा琔षक 牲प से संशोिधत कराया और 灹कािशत कराया जाएगा बशत犇 琈क फ琉स के पूव榁वत牸 संशोधन क琉 तारीख से 6 महीन犘 क琉 अविध के भीतर कोई संशोधन नह牷 琈कया जाएगा । [फा. सं. आरड瀑瀖यू/एनएच-37016/3/2012-एनएचडीपी-IVए] अलकेश शमा榁, संयु榉त सिचव -- 3 of 6 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(ii)] MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 24th September, 2014 S.O. 2492(E).—Whereas, by the notification of the Government of India in the erstwhile Ministry of Shipping, Road Transport and Highways (Department of Road Transport & Highways), number S.O.1096(E), dated 4th August, 2005 and 1051(E), dated 24th April, 2009 issued under section 5 of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956), the Central Government has entrusted National Highway No. 89 in the State of Rajasthan to the State Government of Rajasthan (hereinafter referred to as the “Authority”); And whereas, the Authority has entered into an Agreement on 31.10.2012 (hereinafter referred to as said “Agreement”) with M/s GVR AJMER - NAGAUR TOLLWAY PRIVATE LIMITED, having its Registered Office at VBC Solitaire, 9th & 10th Floors, No. 47, 49 Bazullah Road, T. Nagar, Chennai– 600017, India, (hereinafter referred to as “Concessionaire”), for the bid submitted on 18.01.2012 for the Development of Ajmer-Nagaur section of NH-89 from Km 0.000 [Design Chainage Km 0.000] to Km 161.000 [Design Chainage Km 148.250] of National Highway No. – 89 (Design Length – 148.25 Km) in the State of Rajasthan (hereinafter referred to as the “said section”) by Two-laning with Paved Shoulder through Public Private Partnership on Design, Build, Finance, Operate and Transfer (DBFOT) basis; Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by section 8A of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956), read with Rule 3 of the National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and Collection) Rules, 2008 and Amended upto the date of receipt of the bid (herein referred to as the “Rules”), the Central Government, having regard to the expenditure involved in building, maintenance, management and operation of the said section, interest on the capital invested, reasonable return, the volume of traffic and the period of the Agreement entered into between the Authority and the Concessionaire, hereby notifies that there shall be levied and collected fees on mechanical vehicles for the use of the said section at 60% of the rate specified in column (2) of the Table 1 for net road section length of 115.00 Kms for use of the two lane sections (hereinafter referred to as “base rate”) and at 90% of the rate specified in column (2) of the Table 1 on four bypasses of total length 33.25 Kms on the Types of Vehicles specified in column (1) of the Table 1 below, and authorizes the said Concessionaire to collect and retain the fees on and from the date of commercial operation (COD) and on getting the completion certificate or the provisional certificate, as the case may be, from the Authority or publication of this notification in the Official Gazette, whichever is later, till the termination date as specified in the Agreement; Table 1 Type of Vehicle Base rate of fee per km for the base year 2007- 08 (In Rupees) (1) (2) Car, Jeep, Van or Light Motor Vehicle. 0.65 Light Commercial Vehicle, Light Goods Vehicle or Mini Bus. 1.05 Bus or Truck (Two Axles) 2.20 Three-Axle Commercial Vehicles 2.40 Heavy Construction Machinery (HCM) or Earth Moving Equipment (EME) or Multi Axle Vehicle. (MAV) (four to six axles) 3.45 Oversized Vehicles. (seven or more axles) 4.20 -- 4 of 6 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3 (ii)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 5 1. The fee levied and collected hereunder shall be due and payable at the following Toll Plazas for the distance specified for such Toll Plazas: Location of Toll Plazas (chainage) Length (in Km) for which Fee is payable Rate at which the fee is payable Existing Chainage Km/Ch 33.348 (Design Chainage Km 25.000) on NH-89 (near village Badighati in Nagaur District) 44.65 60% of the base rates mentioned in Table 1 above Existing Chainage Km/Ch 118.936 (Design Chainage Km 111.000) on NH-89 (near village Butati in Nagaur District) 70.35 60% of the base rates mentioned in Table 1 above 2. In addition to the above, the following fee shall also be due and payable for the use of following bypasses, cost of which is specified hereunder and at the following toll plaza(s): Location of Toll Plazas (chainage) Name & Length (in Km) of the bypass for which additional Fee is payable Cost of Bypass/realign ment (Rs. In crore) Rate at which the fee is payable Existing Chainage Km/Ch 33.348 (Design Chainage Km 25.000) on NH-89 (near village Badighati in Nagaur District) Ajmer-Pushkar Bypass of 22.00 Km length taking off at Km 386.341 of NH-8 [Design Chainage Km 0.000] and merging at Km 30.350 of NH-89 [Design Chainage Km 22.000] 86.25 90% of the base rates mentioned in Table 1 above Thanwala Bypass of 3.35 Km length taking off at Km 36.950 of NH-89 [Design Chainage Km 28.600] and merging at Km 39.600 of NH-89 [Design Chainage Km 31.950] 10.21 90% of the base rates mentioned in Table 1 above Existing Chainage Km/Ch 118.936 (Design Chainage Km 111.000) on NH-89 (near village Butati in Nagaur District) Kuchera Bypass of 4.20 Km length taking off at Km 122.150 [Design Chainage Km 114.050] and merging at Km 125.750 of NH-89 [Design Chainage Km 118.250] 12.51 90% of the base rates mentioned in Table 1 above Inana Bypass of 3.70 Km length taking off at Km 148.500 [Design Chainage Km 140.450] and merging at Km 152.800 of NH-89 [Design Chainage Km 144.150] 11.02 90% of the base rates mentioned in Table 1 above 3. The rate of monthly pass for a person who owns a mechanical vehicle registered for non-commercial purposes and resides within a distance of twenty kilometers from the toll plaza is Rs. 225.00 (Rupees Two Hundred Twenty Five only) for the year 2014-15 and is subject to revision every year as per the provisions of the National Highway fee (determination of Rates and collection) Rules, 2008. 4. For multiple journeys on the highway section, passes shall be issued at the following rates namely: Amount Payable Maximum number of one way journeys allowed Period of validity One and half times of the fee for one way journey Two Twenty four hours from the time of payment Two – third of amount of the fee payable for fifty or more single journeys Fifty or more One month from date of payment -- 5 of 6 -- 6 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(ii)] 5. Fee for commercial vehicles (excluding vehicles plying under National Permit) registered in the district where the fee plaza falls shall be 50% of the prescribed rate for that category of vehicle provided no service road or alternative road is available for use of such commercial vehicles. 6. Mechanical vehicle which is loaded in excess of permissible load specified for its category under the Rules, shall not be permitted to use the National Highway for crossing the toll plaza until the excess load has been removed from such mechanical vehicle. The driver or owner or a person in-charge of such overloaded mechanical vehicle shall be liable to pay fee for entering the overloaded vehicles on the National Highway to the Toll Collecting Agency, equal to ten times of the fee applicable to such category of mechanical vehicles under the Rules. 7. All definitions, category of mechanical vehicles, its permissible load, category of exempted vehicles, service road, alternative road etc. shall be as per definitions specified in the said Rule. 8. The fee rates shall be displayed for information of the users in accordance with rules 12 of the said Rules. 9. Based on the base rate of fee per km for the base year 2007-08 as mentioned in Table-1, the actual amount of fee to be charged from the mechanical vehicles and the discounts will be calculated by the Authority on the basis of the completed portion of the length of the project Highway. The same shall be got published by the Authority in at least one newspaper, each in English and vernacular language through the concessionaire and thereafter revised annually in accordance with the rule 5 of the said rules and provision of Concession Agreement and got published, provided that no revision shall be effected with in a period of six months from the date of the preceding revision of fee.
